item.title: Opal Security Raises $23 Million for AI-Native Identity Governance
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
Raising $59 million to date, Opal also announced five senior leadership appointments.

The post Opal Security Raises $23 Million for AI-Native Identity Governance appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: OWASP Incubator Project Helps Developers Find and Fix Vulnerable Dependencies in Seconds
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend
item.description:
CVE Lite CLI is a free, open-source command line tool that scans your projects in seconds and tells you exactly which included packages contain a vulnerability.

The post OWASP Incubator Project Helps Developers Find and Fix Vulnerable Dependencies in Seconds appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: In Other News: Anthropic Maps AI Threats, Unpatched Comodo Flaw, Palantir Chief Eyed for CISA
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News
item.description:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Ultrahuman data leak, The Gentlemen ransomware analysis, Hola Browser bundles miner.

The post In Other News: Anthropic Maps AI Threats, Unpatched Comodo Flaw, Palantir Chief Eyed for CISA appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Hackers Leak DentaQuest Information Impacting 2.6 Million
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
The ShinyHunters extortion group leaked roughly 234 GB of data allegedly stolen from the dental benefits administrator.

The post Hackers Leak DentaQuest Information Impacting 2.6 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Chrome 149 Patches 429 Vulnerabilities
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
Over 100 bugs are critical or high-severity, mainly use-after-free and insufficient validation of untrusted input flaws.

The post Chrome 149 Patches 429 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Industry Reactions to New Trump AI Cybersecurity Executive Order: Feedback Friday
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News
item.description:
Experts commented on the EO’s voluntary nature, the balance between innovation and security, and potential implementation gaps.

The post Industry Reactions to New Trump AI Cybersecurity Executive Order: Feedback Friday appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Five Eyes: Chinese Spies Target Government, Military Staff With Fake Job Opportunities
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
Posing as recruiters on online platforms, Chinese intelligence officers target personnel with access to classified or privileged information.

The post Five Eyes: Chinese Spies Target Government, Military Staff With Fake Job Opportunities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Nightclub Giant RCI Says Data Breach Affects 40,000 Individuals
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs
item.description:
The company detected a network intrusion in March and an investigation showed that some files were stolen during the attack.

The post Nightclub Giant RCI Says Data Breach Affects 40,000 Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cisco Warns of 7th SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in 2026
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs
item.description:
The vulnerability is tracked as CVE-2026-20245 and it can allow arbitrary command execution as root, but no patch yet.

The post Cisco Warns of 7th SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Offroad Emerges From Stealth With $7 Million to Tackle Enterprise Identity Risk
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend
item.description:
As AI agents, machine identities, and third-party applications multiply across enterprises, Offroad is betting autonomous security agents can restore control over an increasingly unmanageable identity landscape.

The post Offroad Emerges From Stealth With $7 Million to Tackle Enterprise Identity Risk appeared first on SecurityWeek.
